meta.name: SPIN Social Phobia Inventory
meta.description: The SPIN is a 17-item self-report scale measuring three dimensions of social anxiety: fear, avoidance, and physiological symptoms.
meta.author: Connor KM, et al. (2000)
meta.reference: Connor KM, et al. Psychometric properties of the Social Phobia Inventory (SPIN). Br J Psychiatry. 2000;176:379-386.
meta.instruction: Please indicate how much the following problems have bothered you during the past week.

questions.text: I am afraid of people in authority.
I am bothered by blushing in front of people.
Parties and social events scare me.
I avoid talking to people I don't know.
Being criticized scares me a lot.
Fear of embarrassment causes me to avoid doing things or speaking to people.
Sweating in front of people causes me distress.
I avoid activities in which I am the center of attention.
I avoid having to give speeches.
I would do anything to avoid being criticized.
Heart palpitations bother me when I am around people.
I am afraid of doing things when people might be watching.
Being embarrassed or looking stupid are among my worst fears.
I avoid speaking to anyone in authority.
Trembling or shaking in front of others is distressing to me.

ranges.label: No Social Anxiety
ranges.description: Your social anxiety level is within the normal range. This indicates that your anxiety levels are within the normal range, and your psychological state is relatively calm.
suggestions: Maintain active social engagement
Continue your confident social style
Continue maintaining your healthy lifestyle and coping strategies
Practice daily relaxation activities such as deep breathing or stretching
Keep up a regular exercise routine

ranges.label: Mild Social Anxiety
ranges.description: You have mild social anxiety symptoms. Mild tension or worry is common in everyday life. Simple self-regulation techniques can often help manage these feelings.
suggestions: Gradually expose yourself to social situations that cause discomfort
Practice relaxation techniques
Challenge negative social thoughts
Learn and practice progressive muscle relaxation
Try 5-10 minutes of mindfulness meditation daily

ranges.label: Moderate Social Anxiety
ranges.description: You have moderate social anxiety symptoms that may affect social functioning. At this level, you may find it difficult to relax, experience frequent worry, and notice effects on sleep or concentration.
suggestions: Consider consulting a mental health professional
Cognitive Behavioral Therapy is highly effective for social anxiety
Consider joining a social skills training group
Consider consulting a counselor or psychiatrist
Cognitive behavioral therapy (CBT) is highly effective for anxiety

ranges.label: Severe Social Anxiety
ranges.description: You have severe social anxiety symptoms. Your anxiety level is elevated and may be accompanied by physical discomfort and persistent tension. Speaking with a professional is recommended.
suggestions: Professional psychological treatment is strongly recommended
Medication may be helpful as an adjunct
Exposure therapy is a well-supported treatment approach
Schedule a professional assessment as soon as possible
A combination of medication and psychotherapy may be beneficial

ranges.label: Very Severe Social Anxiety
ranges.description: You have very severe social anxiety symptoms significantly affecting daily life. Your anxiety level is elevated and may be accompanied by physical discomfort and persistent tension. Speaking with a professional is recommended.
suggestions: Seek help from a psychiatrist or mental health professional as soon as possible
A combined treatment approach may be most effective
Do not let social anxiety prevent you from seeking help
Schedule a professional assessment as soon as possible
A combination of medication and psychotherapy may be beneficial

fear.label: Low Fear
fear.description: Social fear symptoms are mild. Fear in social situations is not prominent. This indicates that your anxiety levels are within the normal range, and your psychological state is relatively calm.
suggestions: Continue maintaining your healthy lifestyle and coping strategies
Practice daily relaxation activities such as deep breathing or stretching
Keep up a regular exercise routine
Cultivate mindful awareness habits
Maintain strong social connections

fear.label: Moderate Fear
fear.description: Some degree of social fear is present. Certain social situations may cause notable discomfort. At this level, you may find it difficult to relax, experience frequent worry, and notice effects on sleep or concentration.
suggestions: Consider consulting a counselor or psychiatrist
Cognitive behavioral therapy (CBT) is highly effective for anxiety
Practice relaxation techniques daily and maintain regular exercise
Identify and reduce avoidance behaviors; gradually face anxiety-provoking situations
Maintain regular sleep and exercise habits

fear.label: High Fear
fear.description: Social fear symptoms are severe. Professional help is recommended. Your anxiety level is elevated and may be accompanied by physical discomfort and persistent tension. Speaking with a professional is recommended.
suggestions: Schedule a professional assessment as soon as possible
A combination of medication and psychotherapy may be beneficial
Learn to recognize and manage panic attacks
Avoid avoidance behavior; gradually face fears under professional guidance
Ensure you have a reliable support system

avoidance.label: Low Avoidance
avoidance.description: Social avoidance behavior is minimal. You can participate in social activities well. This indicates that you are not currently showing significant post-traumatic stress reactions, and your psychological recovery is progressing well.
suggestions: Continue maintaining your good psychological health
Stay emotionally connected with friends and family
Focus on personal growth and positive experiences
Remember to seek support if you face difficult events in the future

avoidance.label: Moderate Avoidance
avoidance.description: Some social avoidance is present. You may tend to escape certain social situations. At this level, trauma responses may include flashbacks, avoidance behaviors, or emotional numbness that affect daily life and relationships.
suggestions: Consider consulting a professional trained in trauma therapy
Trauma-Focused CBT (TF-CBT) or EMDR may be helpful
Build a sense of safety and daily routine
Learn emotional regulation and coping skills
Avoid using alcohol or substances to cope

avoidance.label: High Avoidance
avoidance.description: Social avoidance is significant, seriously affecting social participation. Professional help is recommended. Your trauma-related reactions are quite pronounced and may be significantly impacting your functioning. Professional trauma-focused support is recommended.
suggestions: Contact a trauma therapy specialist as soon as possible
Process traumatic memories in a safe environment
Develop a safety plan and crisis coping strategies
Ensure you have a supportive social network
Follow professional guidance for a gradual recovery process

physiological.label: Low Physiological Response
physiological.description: Physiological reactions in social situations are mild. This indicates that your anxiety levels are within the normal range, and your psychological state is relatively calm.
suggestions: Continue maintaining your healthy lifestyle and coping strategies
Practice daily relaxation activities such as deep breathing or stretching
Keep up a regular exercise routine
Cultivate mindful awareness habits
Maintain strong social connections

physiological.label: Moderate Physiological Response
physiological.description: Some physiological discomfort in social situations, such as rapid heartbeat or sweating. At this level, you may find it difficult to relax, experience frequent worry, and notice effects on sleep or concentration.
suggestions: Consider consulting a counselor or psychiatrist
Cognitive behavioral therapy (CBT) is highly effective for anxiety
Practice relaxation techniques daily and maintain regular exercise
Identify and reduce avoidance behaviors; gradually face anxiety-provoking situations
Maintain regular sleep and exercise habits

physiological.label: Strong Physiological Response
physiological.description: Physiological reactions in social situations are intense. Relaxation techniques and professional help are recommended. Your anxiety level is elevated and may be accompanied by physical discomfort and persistent tension. Speaking with a professional is recommended.
suggestions: Schedule a professional assessment as soon as possible
A combination of medication and psychotherapy may be beneficial
Learn to recognize and manage panic attacks
Avoid avoidance behavior; gradually face fears under professional guidance
Ensure you have a reliable support system
